machine.name: localai/personal_health_baseline
machine.description: Personal health state classifier. Reads three band sensors — heart rate ok, HRV ok, sleep ok — from the Perception Engine and asserts one of four mutually exclusive health states: thriving, balanced, watch, or attention. Structurally parallel to rag_corrective_cycle: all sequences are isInitial and fire in OR arbitration; exactly one state fires per step because the four guards partition the [hr.ok × hrv.ok × sleep.ok] space.

localai_health_hr_ok.description: Heart rate in nominal band [60, 100] bpm → 1.0; outside → 0.0

stateLogic.thriving: hr=OK AND hrv=OK AND sleep=OK — all three baseline pillars healthy
stateLogic.balanced: hr=OK AND hrv=OK AND sleep=LOW — vitals good but insufficient sleep
stateLogic.watch: hr=OK AND hrv=LOW — cardiovascular nominal but recovery is poor; sleep may be anything
stateLogic.attention: hr=LOW/HIGH — heart rate outside nominal range; flag for health check-in
stateLogic.mutualExclusivity: thriving ⊃ balanced ⊃ watch ⊃ attention via the hr/hrv/sleep split; no two fire in the same step

localAIPromptHints.thriving: All baseline health metrics are in nominal range. The user is thriving physically.
localAIPromptHints.balanced: Cardiovascular metrics are healthy but sleep is below target. Gently prompt rest.
localAIPromptHints.watch: Heart rate is nominal but HRV indicates low recovery. Suggest lighter activity today.
localAIPromptHints.attention: Heart rate is outside the nominal range. Recommend a health check-in.

sequences.name: Thriving: hr=OK AND hrv=OK AND sleep=OK

metadata.description: Fires when all three health band sensors are in their nominal range. The most positive state — no action warranted.
All health baseline pillars nominal — thriving
metadata.promptHint: All baseline health metrics are in nominal range.

metadata.name: THRIVING_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: hr_ok HIGH (>=0.5); element[1]: hrv_ok HIGH (>=0.5); element[2]: sleep_ok HIGH (>=0.5); element[3]: wildcard

sequences.name: Balanced: hr=OK AND hrv=OK AND sleep=LOW

metadata.description: Fires when heart rate and HRV are both nominal but sleep duration is below target. Vitals are healthy but rest is lacking — a gentle nudge toward better sleep hygiene.
Cardiovascular metrics nominal; sleep below target — balanced
metadata.promptHint: Cardiovascular metrics are healthy but sleep is below target. Gently prompt rest.

metadata.name: BALANCED_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: hr_ok HIGH; element[1]: hrv_ok HIGH; element[2]: sleep_ok LOW (<0.5, GTE split at 0.5 with value=0); element[3]: wildcard. Mutually exclusive with thriving because sleep_ok is LOW here.
Fires when heart rate is nominal but HRV indicates poor recovery. Sleep may be anything. Suggests lighter activity and recovery focus today.
Heart rate nominal but HRV indicates low recovery — watch
metadata.promptHint: Heart rate is nominal but HRV indicates low recovery. Suggest lighter activity today.

metadata.name: WATCH_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: hr_ok HIGH; element[1]: hrv_ok LOW; elements[2-3]: wildcard. Mutually exclusive with thriving/balanced because hrv_ok is LOW.
Fires when heart rate is outside the nominal [60, 100] bpm band. This is the highest-priority state — cardiovascular signal warrants a health check-in regardless of HRV or sleep.
Heart rate outside nominal band — attention required
metadata.promptHint: Heart rate is outside the nominal range. Recommend a health check-in.

metadata.name: ATTENTION_CHECK
metadata.description: element[0]: hr_ok LOW (<0.5, GTE split at 0.5 with value=0); elements[1-3]: wildcard. Mutually exclusive with thriving/balanced/watch because they all require hr_ok HIGH.

inputSequences.name: All nominal — thriving
inputSequences.description: 80 bpm (hr.ok=1.0), HRV=45ms (hrv.ok=1.0), sleep=7.5h (sleep.ok=1.0). All three sensors HIGH. Should assert thriving [1,0,0,0].

metadata.notes: hr.ok=1.0 (≥0.5 HIGH), hrv.ok=1.0 (≥0.5 HIGH), sleep.ok=1.0 (≥0.5 HIGH) → thriving fires

inputSequences.name: Good vitals, poor sleep — balanced
inputSequences.description: 75 bpm (hr.ok=1.0), HRV=38ms (hrv.ok=1.0), sleep=5.5h (sleep.ok=0.0). HR and HRV nominal but sleep below 6.5h target. Should assert balanced [0,1,0,0].

metadata.notes: hr.ok HIGH, hrv.ok HIGH, sleep.ok=0.0 (<0.5 LOW) → balanced fires

inputSequences.name: HR nominal but poor recovery — watch
inputSequences.description: 70 bpm (hr.ok=1.0), HRV=18ms (hrv.ok=0.0), sleep=6.0h (sleep.ok=0.0). HR fine but HRV signals poor recovery. Should assert watch [0,0,1,0].

metadata.notes: hr.ok HIGH, hrv.ok=0.0 (<0.5 LOW) → watch fires; sleep is also low but watch fires first in state space

inputSequences.name: HR out of range — attention
inputSequences.description: 105 bpm (hr.ok=0.0 — above 100bpm ceiling), HRV and sleep irrelevant. Heart rate flag is highest priority. Should assert attention [0,0,0,1].

metadata.notes: hr.ok=0.0 (<0.5 LOW) → attention fires regardless of other sensors
